fields.status?: string; optional; working/finished. Default is working. Use finished only when the job is complete and no more runtime interaction is needed; then final_answer is shown as the closing user response.
fields.report_job_progress?: string; progress report for multi-round tasks so the user can see current status.
fields.final_answer?: string; final user-facing answer for the current task. If you include final_answer, also include status:"finished".
fields.next_actions?: array<object>; required when status is working. Refer to Tools And Skills for available actions. With status finished and final_answer, next_actions may contain one final run_bash command whose exit code decides whether to really finish; if nonzero, runtime ignores final_answer and returns the command result for continued work.

thought?.content: string; optional private reasoning draft. You can use as you need. Not shown to the user.
thought?.keep_in_context: boolean; optional. Defaults to false. Set to true ONLY when the current reasoning contains complex state machines, logic dependencies, or temporary data variables that subsequent actions cannot progress without. For simple task, leave it false.

action_object_spec.action: string; required. Tool name exactly as listed in the Available tool capabilities catalog. Do not invent names.
action_object_spec.intent: string; required. Concise user-visible reason for the action.
action_object_spec.args: object; required. Put every tool parameter as a JSON field inside args, for example {"type":"durable","op":"query","query":"project codename","limit":5}. Refer to tool's manifest for details.
